meta.title: The State of Revenue on Solana

transcript: First and foremost, thank you to the Solana Foundation for hosting this event in such an incredible event, uh, venue. It's truly an honor to be on stage speaking with you all today. Good morning, good afternoon, good evening. I have no sense of time. I got in last night, bad decision. Um, but today I want to be talking about the state of revenue on Solana. And so I want to focus on sort of two prongs of that. Both at the application layer, so the businesses that are building on-chain and generating revenue, as well as what that means for the base layer, so Solana itself. Um, and when I think about that relationship, it's sort of, uh, it kind of comes back to this idea of the Everything Chain. I think Solana has been called, uh, many variations of 'the blank chain', most of them not too kind over the over the years, but, um, the Everything Chain is the most compelling thesis of a blockchain in my mind. And I would describe that pretty succinctly as being the hub for all of global finance. And so we'll dive into what exactly gets us there. Um, but I want to kick things off by looking at the application layer. So in April of 2024, Solana overtook the position, the pole position of becoming the chain with the most applications built atop of it that are generating revenue, and it has maintained that lead ever since. And when we look at the composition of the applications or the businesses and the sectors they reside in, uh, it tells a very straightforward story of it's trading. That is the the use case that the users of 2025 have been excited about and participating in. Um, whether that's at the application layer in the trading and wallet side of things, so being like a terminal or anything that enables users to get, um, a trading experience, whether that be the exchanges on spot or perpetuals. Um, the asset issuers, so obviously pump.fun and memecoins have been, uh, the sort of the talk of the town for the last 24 months. Or even transaction landing services that enable traders and users, uh, to have a better, uh, transaction success rate. All of these things, uh, have really catalyzed a couple core principles for Solana, and that has brought in users, assets, liquidity, uh, and and all of the components to build a viable ecosystem that has really excited developers to come build here. And expand beyond just the the assets of today and start bringing in the assets of tomorrow, whether that be more, uh, professionalized to- tokenized equities, credit, uh, instruments or other, uh, financial instruments. Or whether that be continuing to innovate on the crypto-native side of things with, um, uh, the ICM meta or things like MetaDAO bringing in, uh, ownership tokens onto Solana. And this relationship doesn't only exist at the app layer. For Solana itself, it's very interesting to see the impact of having a trading, uh, exchange, exchanges built on top of you. 60% of all transaction fees, so base and priority fees, that occur on Solana include or are come from transactions that include a trade. Right, so 60% of all transaction fees, uh, come from transactions that include a a DEX swap, specifically on the spot side of things. This data is for the month of November, but the trend holds for much longer than this. And if you include perpetuals here as well, uh, the number only goes higher. And when we zoom in on what that has meant for the base layer, uh, Prop AMMs. If you aren't familiar, we can dive into a quick definition, uh, but I can promise you by the end of this conference you will almost certainly, uh, be tired of hearing this phrase, but real quick, uh, Prop AMMs are a DEX, uh, DEX design iteration where market makers are able to provide proprietary liquidity, uh, and plug directly into DEX aggregators as opposed to having their own frontend. And can service active liquidity management through, uh, a price oracle that responds to move the, uh, move the the price of the pool without requiring a trade to pass through it. Um, and it's it these have been tremendously successful, especially over the last 12 or so months, where, uh, 60% of spot trading volume now moves over Prop AMM rails. Uh, and when we look at how effective they are at, uh, avoiding toxic flow, which is the the kind of the key to being a a profitable market maker, uh, you see that they are quite effective. So the top three Prop AMMs are the purple line here. Just took the average of their markouts over a 45-day period, uh, starting October 1st. And the green line here is the traditional DEXes or constant product or concentrated liquidity AMMs. And we see on the SOL-USDC pair, Prop AMMs are able to perform at a much higher level, um, because they are able to flow segment run flow segmentation and update their liquidity much more actively and therefore avoid a lot of that toxic flow that has been harmful to LPs of the past. And when we look at that liquidity management, one of the most interesting things is how frequent they are able to do this. So 20% of Solana transactions today are from these oracle updates. And that sort of translates directly into 175 transactions per per second on Solana are being generated by market makers competing to provide users with the best price. That is what the Everything Chain is. And today this is on about 30 or 50 different pairs of crypto-native assets, but you can imagine a world where that 10, 20, 50Xs and there's now 5,000 different assets that are traded on Solana, and market makers are competing to provide them the best quotes. And to me, that is a a credible path to satiating the demand generated by the client teams. I think it was last Breakpoint where the idea of 1 million TPS really got exciting. Uh, and it's also interesting to note that while there are plenty of these transactions, uh, they're extremely optimized and made to be extremely cheap, uh, and that's why they're so effective at what they do. So those 20% of transactions translate to about 2% of total transaction fees and out-of-protocol tips paid, uh, to transact. And so when we look at putting this in the context of the base layer, uh, Solana's 2024 and 2025, as this chart notes, is is almost hilariously good in context to the previous couple of years. Uh, and when we when we think about what has happened over that period, again we see this has created this user base, uh, of of users that are excited to be on-chain and want to be on-chain and want to interact with different applications and trade assets. And that happened in 2024 and was solidified as not a one-time event in 2025. But being pragmatic, when we look at this chart, uh, there's kind of kind of two things that jump out at me. One is the enormous amount of work that the client teams, Anza and Firedancer, as well as third-party teams building in the infrastructure layer as well, have done to do everything in their power to make the chain more efficient and more effective at landing and processing transactions. And the second thing is, uh, sort of the reality that if you look at the six-month period starting in Q4 of 2024 and ending in Q1 of 2025, you see that that is roughly 50% of all REV generated over this two-year period. And so when I think about, well what is going to be the catalyst that pushes Solana forward from here, it's the idea of being the Everything Chain. Again, being the place where, uh, being the hub for all of financial activity across the world. That's every asset across every market in any type of financial instrument, whether that be spot, perpetuals, options, or anything else. Right, I think to me this is the most compelling value proposition for any blockchain to be going after, and Solana is continually putting itself in the position to compete here. Um, and if you if this is achieved, I think the native asset of this, uh, of this blockchain becomes incredibly compelling because, uh, you can imagine if all of the world's financial activity is happening on Solana, uh, as the economic output of the world increases, so does the transactions that happen on-chain, and therefore the yield that the token generates, whether that's through a burn of transaction fees or a distribution of staking rewards. And that is what keeps me logging in every day, that is what keep keeps me coming back to the industry. Um, and so I I guess I would leave it here today with encouraging the Solana community to to continue on the pursuit of the end of being the Everything Chain. Thank you.

# MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025
MetaMask is offering free Solana ID minting until December 22, unlocking exclusive Solana ecosystem perks for users.
![Kelvin Munene](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/d4ea74978aea7ee299447dc24a59aa1dac1fdc2a31a46fbf19da2cdce3d322bd?s=32&r=g)By[Kelvin Munene](https://coincentral.com/author/kelvin/)December 8, 2025**4 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/metamask-1.png "MetaMask Announces Free Solana ID Minting for Users Before Breakpoint 2025")
## TLDR
* MetaMask users can mint a Solana ID for free until December 22, 2025.
* The initiative provides users access to perks like staking boosts and rewards.
* Solana partners like Orca and Sanctum offer exclusive benefits for Solana ID holders.
* The program aims to increase wallet adoption and engagement in Solana apps.
MetaMaskhas announced a new initiative that offers users the chance to mint a free Solana ID. This special promotion is available until December 22, 2025. The move is part of a broader partnership between MetaMask and Solana Identity, ahead of Solana’s annual developer conference, Breakpoint 2025. This initiative is designed to encourage users to engage more with Solana’s ecosystem and to provide them with exclusive benefits.
**Mint a Solana ID to Unlock Exclusive Ecosystem Perks**
By minting a Solana ID using MetaMask,[users](https://x.com/MetaMask/status/1998060127950409942?s=20)can unlock various perks across the Solana blockchain ecosystem. These include rewards like staking boosts, exclusive swaps, and access to discounts. Additionally, users will be eligible to participate in raffles and earn rewards from several key partners within the Solana network. Notable Solana-based platforms involved in the program include Orca, SNS, Sanctum, Cudis, and Play Solana.
MetaMask users will not only gain access to these platform-specific rewards but will also be able to explore further opportunities within the Solana ecosystem. These perks are meant to deepen user engagement and increase wallet adoption among MetaMask users who are interacting with Solana applications.
**How the Solana ID Minting Works**
Minting a Solana ID via MetaMask is straightforward. Users simply need to connect their[MetaMask](https://coincentral.com/metamask-introduces-musd-to-simplify-crypto-payments-and-defi-access/)wallet to the Solana Identity platform and follow the steps to mint their Solana ID. The process is free, and users will retain the ID throughout their interactions with Solana-based services. Once minted, the Solana ID will be linked to the user’s MetaMask wallet, allowing them to access the various ecosystem benefits.
The initiative aims to bring together the strengths of MetaMask’s popular walle

Solomon Labs Caps $SOLO Token Raise at $8M Despite $102M in Commitments
# Solomon Labs Caps $SOLO Token Raise at $8M Despite $102M in Commitments
Business
[Business](https://www.blocmates.com/news-tags/business)
DeFi
[DeFi](https://www.blocmates.com/news-tags/defi)
Markets
[Markets](https://www.blocmates.com/news-tags/markets)
Coins
[Coins](https://www.blocmates.com/news-tags/coins)
by
Edyme
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64e2d11370e5eca9bb7f2084/651bb617fcd5631637f3d617_facebook_blue.avif)](#)[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64e2d11370e5eca9bb7f2084/64e6d8976b97e6efd72020b9_twitter_blue.svg)](#)[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64e2d11370e5eca9bb7f2084/64e6d8976b97e6efd72020ba_linkedIn_blue.svg)](#)
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64e2d11370e5eca9bb7f2084/64e6d8976b97e6efd72020bb_link_blue.svg)](#)
Copied!
November 19, 2025
![The demand translated into 6,600+ contributors and commitments that surpassed the target by more than 50x](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64e2d11370e5eca9bb7f2087/691de7693ed5774d1b513120_230.webp)
Solomon Labs’ public token sale wrapped up with far more demand than expected, pulling in over $102 million in commitments despite targeting just $2 million at launch.
The offering, held on MetaDAO from November 15 to 18, centered on the protocol’s governance token, $SOLO, and quickly became one of the most competitive raises to hit Solana this year.
Instead of scaling the round to match demand, the team opted to cap the raise at $8 million, issuing automatic refunds to participants before shifting focus to mainnet deployment.
## **How a stablecoin protocol pulled in $100M+ of demand**
Solomon Labs is building USDv, a Solana-native stablecoin designed to automatically earn yield without rebasing. The idea is: instead of users depositing stablecoins into external lending platforms, USDv embeds yield generation directly into the asset, positioning itself as “digital cash that earns.”
According to the project, its beta rate sits around 20.9% APY, though returns may change as the system scales. The token sale leaned on MetaDAO’s “ownership coin” model, where tokens represent participation in protocol decisions rather than fixed allocations tied to traditional governance rules.
The demand translated into 6,600+ contributors and commitments that surpassed the target by more than 50x. With the raise capped at $8 million, tokens priced at $0.80, placing $SOLO at a roughly $20.6 million fully diluted valuation.
> The Solomon ICO has officially concluded.
> We raised $102,932,673 from 6,603 contributors.
> Huge thank you to everyone who backed us.

tags.impact_reason: Provides a solid update on Solana's application layer revenue and trading volume, but lacks immediate, groundbreaking news.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline data monolith as the Semantic Anchor to represent the Solana base layer acting as a powerful, centralized hub for global finance, as described in the 'Everything Chain' thesis. The visual of it 'devouring' streams of light directly translates the headline's core action of Prop AMMs consuming liquidity. For the diegetic text, I selected '60%' from the headline, as it's the single most impactful statistic in the story. Integrating it as a glowing, holographic system metric on the monolith's surface makes it feel like an intrinsic part of this high-tech financial machine.
visual_idea.description: A colossal, dark crystalline monolith, representing the Solana network, stands in a dark, abstract digital space. Rivers of liquid, neon-purple and cyan light—representing trading volume—are being aggressively siphoned into glowing intake ports along its surface, creating a vortex of data. Etched into a translucent facet of the monolith, the text '60%' glows intensely from within, like a critical system status display. The background is a vast, dark void with faint grid lines, emphasizing the scale of this financial hub.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A colossal, dark crystalline monolith, representing the Solana network, stands monumentally in a dark, abstract digital space. Its hyperrealistic, faceted surface is composed of deeply reflective, realistic crystalline materials, catching and refracting what little light exists. Rivers of liquid, intensely glowing neon-purple and cyan light, representing trading volume, are being aggressively siphoned into numerous glowing intake ports along its surface. These streams of liquid data create a dynamic vortex effect, with wet, detailed textures and dramatic streaks of light. Etched deeply into a translucent facet of the monolith, the text '60%' glows intensely from within, like a critical system status display, emitting a soft, internal light. The background is a vast, dark void with faint, almost holographic grid lines, emphasizing the epic scale of this financial singularity. Volumetric lighting casts deep shadows and high-contrast light, creating a high-contrast cyber-noir atmosphere. The composition emphasizes the immense height and monumental presence of the monolith, with significant negative space above, drawing the eye upwards. The entire scene is rendered with ultra-realistic lighting and cinematic contrast, showcasing intricate architectural details and reflective surfaces. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, volumetric lighting, cinematic, ultra-sharp digital render, sci-fi aesthetic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline data monolith as the Semantic Anchor to represent the Solana base layer acting as a powerful, centralized hub for global finance, as described in the 'Everything Chain' thesis. The visual of it 'devouring' streams of light directly translates the headline's core action of Prop AMMs consuming liquidity. For the diegetic text, I selected '60%' from the headline, as it's the single most impactful statistic in the story. Integrating it as a glowing, holographic system metric on the monolith's surface makes it feel like an intrinsic part of this high-tech financial machine.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focused on the polished, crystalline surface of the monolith. A turbulent torrent of liquid purple and cyan light crashes and flows into a glowing data port. The focus is on the immense energy and the beautiful, chaotic reflection of the data streams on the monolith's dark, geometric surface. No text is visible in this close-up.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A breathtaking, hyper-detailed macro shot, perfectly centered and symmetrical, filling the square frame (1:1 aspect ratio). The focus is sharply on the pristine, polished, dark crystalline surface of an immense geometric monolith. A turbulent, mesmerizing torrent of liquid light, shimmering in vibrant electric purple and cyan hues, dynamically crashes and flows directly into a glowing, intricate data port embedded within the monolith. Emphasize the immense, raw energy radiating from this interaction, creating beautiful, chaotic reflections of the data streams that dance across the dark, geometric surface of the monolith. Volumetric lighting illuminates the liquid data streams and the glowing port, casting deep shadows and brilliant highlights, showcasing intricate textural details across the entire frame. No text is visible in this close-up. 8k, octane render, raytracing, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, cinematic contrast, hyper-detailed textures, sci-fi aesthetic, dramatic lighting, sharp focus, f/1.8

editorial.headline: Prop AMMs Devour 60% of Solana Spot Volume as 'Everything Chain' Thesis Matures
editorial.standfirst: Blockworks' Dan Smith reveals how proprietary market makers have silently taken over Solana's liquidity layer, driving the network toward a 1 million TPS future powered by Internet Capital Markets.
key_takeaways: Solana has maintained the #1 spot for application revenue since April 2024, with the Q4 2024–Q1 2025 period generating 50% of the network's total two-year revenue.
Proprietary AMMs (Prop AMMs) now capture 60% of spot trading volume, generating 175 TPS (20% of network traffic) while consuming just 2% of total fees.
The 'Internet Capital Markets' (ICM) meta, spearheaded by protocols like MetaDAO, is shifting the ecosystem from memecoins to structured ownership of 5,000+ global assets.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative that Solana is merely a casino for memecoins is dead; the data proves it is evolving into the high-frequency trading hub of global finance. In his "State of Revenue" address at Breakpoint 2025, **Blockworks Research** Head of Data Dan Smith presented a thesis that redefines the network's value proposition: Solana is the "Everything Chain." The proof lies in the revenue. Since taking the pole position in April 2024, Solana has consistently led all chains in application-layer revenue, with a staggering 50% of the last two years' total revenue generated in just the six-month window between Q4 2024 and Q1 2025.

The real alpha, however, is hidden in the market structure. A quiet revolution known as **Proprietary Automated Market Makers (Prop AMMs)** has completely cannibalized traditional liquidity models. Unlike constant product AMMs (like Uniswap v2) that expose passive liquidity providers to toxic flow and arbitrage, Prop AMMs allow professional market makers to manage liquidity off-chain and update quotes on-chain via high-frequency oracles. Smith's data reveals that these entities now control **60% of all spot trading volume** on Solana. They are vastly superior at avoiding toxic flow, as evidenced by 45-day markout data on the SOL-USDC pair, where Prop AMMs consistently outperformed traditional DEXs by segmenting flow and updating prices aggressively.

This shift has profound implications for Solana's scalability and economic model. Prop AMMs are currently generating roughly **175 transactions per second**—accounting for 20% of all network transactions—yet they are so optimized that they contribute only 2% to total transaction fees. This efficiency is the precursor to the "1 million TPS" goal; the volume won't come from users clicking buttons, but from market makers competing to provide the tightest quotes across thousands of assets. As the ecosystem pivots toward the **Internet Capital Markets (ICM)** meta—referenced by Smith alongside **MetaDAO**—the network is preparing to host not just crypto-native tokens, but tokenized equities, credit, and thousands of other financial instruments.

For the institutional investor, the endgame is clear. If Solana succeeds in becoming the execution layer for all global finance, the value accrual to the native token becomes undeniable. As economic output increases, so does the yield generated from transaction fees and staking rewards. We are witnessing the industrialization of on-chain finance, moving from the chaotic speculation of 2024 to the structured, high-performance infrastructure of 2025.
